topic.what: Semantic Gateway architecture for securing autonomous AI agents in enterprise systems using Model Context Protocol
topic.how: Three-layer Zero-Trust model (Semantic Firewall, Tool-Level RBAC, Cryptographic HITL) plus Enabledness-Preserving Abstractions and greybox semantic fuzzing adapted from smart contract verification
topic.why_matters: Probabilistic LLMs break classical software validation and access control; enterprises adopting agentic AI face emergent vulnerabilities (indirect prompt injection, semantic BOLA, unpredictable state transitions) that require formal verification of agent behavior

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of hidden unauthorized state transitions discovered by fuzzing
characteristics.vs_baseline: vs 0% discovery with standard integration tests
characteristics.context: Fuzzing campaign against agent enabled-tool graphs

characteristics.what_it_measures: Reduction in boilerplate authorization and validation code
Number of multi-turn fuzzing sequences executed
Number of distinct verification layers in Zero-Trust model
characteristics.context: Defense-in-depth architecture: Semantic Firewall, Tool RBAC, HITL

characteristics.what_it_measures: Ability to detect emergent state transitions undetectable by static analysis
characteristics.vs_baseline: vs static code analysis and standard integration tests
characteristics.context: Key claim: dynamic formal verification is necessary for agentic enterprise systems

novelty: First adaptation of Enabledness-Preserving Abstractions from blockchain smart contracts to AI agent verification
First formal treatment of autonomous agents as stochastic state-transition systems with auditable enabled-tool graphs
First semantic fuzzing methodology for enterprise agentic systems with 100% hidden transition discovery
Three-layer Zero-Trust model combining pre-inference firewall, tool-level RBAC, and cryptographic HITL
open_problems: Scalability of EPA abstractions for agents with thousands of tools
Formal verification of multi-agent coordination and communication
Automated policy synthesis from natural language security requirements
Real-time verification for high-throughput agentic workloads
